product: Farm-direct regenerative grocery store with hub-and-collect model + equity crowdfunding campaign

scoring_context.product: FarmThru — a new kind of grocery store. Farm-direct, regenerative, priced for everyday families. Hub-and-collect model (Brookvale hub, Monday-Friday). 50+ farms across NSW. Co-founded by Rachel Ward. Currently running pre-launch equity crowdfunding campaign on Birchal (July 2026).
scoring_context.brand_positioning: FarmThru is a new kind of grocery store — farm-direct, regenerative, and priced for everyday families — with hubs designed to keep costs low and bring the farm closer to your door.
scoring_context.tagline: We'll show you where your food comes from.
scoring_context.audience: Sydney households. Four segments: (1) Conscious Family — parents 30-45, Northern Beaches, health-conscious, values-driven, time-poor. Lead with quality and provenance. (2) Foodie/Quality Seeker — 25-55, passionate cooks. Lead with product stories, farm detail. (3) Pragmatic Switcher — 30-50, mainstream shoppers currently at Coles/Woolies. Lead with value, convenience, better product. (4) Rachel Ward Audience — 40+, trust her voice. Lead with Rachel's personal connection.
key_motivations: Access — great local food, finally within reach for everyday families
Transparency — know exactly where food comes from, who grew it, how it was raised
Quality — regenerative, seasonal, real food from named farms
Value — bulk buying direct means honest prices without farms earning less
Community — being part of rebuilding the connection between growers and eaters
Investment — owning a piece of a business they believe in (CFE campaign only)
scoring_context.brand_values: Honest, Warm, Confident, Grounded, Never Preachy. Lead with the food, not the model. Communication hierarchy: (1) Great products, (2) Human relationships, (3) Reconnection to food, (4) Learning, (5) Model/tech last.
scoring_context.campaign_context: Pre-launch equity crowdfunding via Birchal. Content types: (1) Brand/product ads — pure grocery awareness, no investment messaging, (2) CFE waitlist ads — investment angle, waitlist CTA, (3) CFE campaign ads — investment is live, invest CTA. All investment content must comply with ASIC RG 234. VIP deposit ($5 refundable) messaging belongs on landing pages and emails, NOT ads.
scoring_context.success_looks_like: Reader understands FarmThru is a grocery store where you can get great food from real farms at honest prices. They want to find their nearest hub. For CFE content: reader feels informed, trusts the brand, joins the waitlist.

receptionist_test_questions: Where does the food come from?
How is this different from the supermarket?
How do I invest / join the waitlist?
